rootCauseAnalysis.summary: Pod cannot schedule because its only eligible node (kubernaut-demo-worker, matching nodeSelector kubernaut.ai/demo-taint-target=true) has a NoSchedule taint (maintenance=scheduled) that the pod has no toleration for. Both Deployment replicas are 100% unavailable as a result.
contributingFactors: Deployment batch-processor uses nodeSelector kubernaut.ai/demo-taint-target=true, which only matches a single node: kubernaut-demo-worker
kubernaut-demo-worker has a NoSchedule taint (maintenance=scheduled) applied at 2026-04-23T16:33:46Z — exactly when the pod was created
The pod spec has no toleration for the maintenance=scheduled taint, making all 3 cluster nodes ineligible
The remaining 2 nodes (control-plane and worker2) are excluded because they lack the kubernaut.ai/demo-taint-target=true label
Both replicas of the Deployment are affected, resulting in 100% unavailability (unavailableReplicas=2)
No prior remediation history exists for this Deployment, suggesting this is a first-time or freshly deployed workload encountering a pre-planned maintenance taint

kaResponse.analysis: Pod cannot schedule because its only eligible node (kubernaut-demo-worker, matching nodeSelector kubernaut.ai/demo-taint-target=true) has a NoSchedule taint (maintenance=scheduled) that the pod has no toleration for. Both Deployment replicas are 100% unavailable as a result.
